subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and repeated definition of âchangeâ linked to Alibabaâs acquisition context
Strong organization-specific examples (market-share, revenue, cost pressures) anchoring the concept
Effective use of business management guide definitions to frame methodology
weaknesses: Concept integration is less evident in some analytical sections (methodology, financial table discussion)
Could more consistently restate âchangeâ in each analysis subsection to sustain integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA demonstrates a strong and partially integrated understanding of the key concept of change, with clear analytical links to Alibabaâs acquisition throughout most of the assessment. References are sometimes clustered in only a few sections rather than woven continuously, aligning with band 4.

strengths: Sources cover a range of perspectives including annual reports and industry articles
Inclusion of a timely TechCrunch report provides up-to-date context
weaknesses: Eight documents exceed the rubricâs 3â5 recommendation, diluting focus
Limited depth of evidence extracted from multiple sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although sources are generally relevant, the excessive number of documents reduces the depth and coherence of evidence. This aligns most closely with band 1 for the number of documents, with only marginal relevance depth.

strengths: Use of Force-Field Analysis and Gross Profit Margin demonstrates some selection of business tools
Tools are applied directly to Alibabaâs acquisition outcomes
weaknesses: Limited range of tools â no complementary analytical frameworks (e.g., ratio comparisons)
Methodology lacks detail on data selection criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA selects two appropriate tools and applies them to the research question, but the breadth and rigor of application are limited. This performance sits at the top of band 2 moving toward band 3 due to clear relevance, justifying 2/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of revenue, cost, market-share and user data to explain performance shifts
Mostly coherent analysis linking drivers, restraints and financial trends to acquisition impact
weaknesses: Force-field weights are not explicitly labeled, reducing transparency
Causal attribution could be strengthened by isolating Kaola-specific cost changes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is sufficient and the IA offers mostly effective analysis with integration of internal and external factors. Some depth and transparency are lacking, placing this at band 4.

strengths: Conclusions fully reflect key findings (revenue growth, market-share increase, cost pressures)
Explicitly and directly answers the research question with evidenceâbacked judgments
weaknesses: Does not discuss limitations or uncertainties of the analysis
Could reflect on data constraints to strengthen critical evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with the evidence presented and explicitly addresses the research question, meeting all criteria for band 3.

weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers for quick navigation
Some headings could better signal how each section ties back to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure is apparent but has minor omissions (missing page numbers, slightly under-developed linkage between background context and RQ), aligning with band 1 for structure.

strengths: All required IA elements (title page, word count, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references) are present
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting across DOIs and URLs
Lack of inline citations for numerical data reduces traceability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While all elements are included, inconsistencies in citation style and missing inline references limit professionalism, placing presentation at band 1.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure (introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references) and uses clear sub-headings and tables, meeting the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2).

Most documents are clearly related to the acquisition (annual report, TechCrunch article, academic papers) but the depth of evidence extracted is modestâonly one table of financial data and a few headline market-share figures are used.  The references provide relevance but limited analytical richness, aligning with âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ in band 2.
All required IA elementsâtitle page with RQ, word count, methodology, analysis, conclusion and reference listâare present, earning the full 2 marks for required elements.

Eight separate sources are cited, which exceeds the rubricâs 3-5 range; the IA therefore falls into the âonly 1-2 documents or more than 5 documentsâ category.  Consequently the strand is limited to band 1 for number of documents.

Both tools are applied to the research question: the Force-Field matrix is custom-built for Alibaba/Kaola and the GPM calculation is interpreted to discuss revenue growth versus margin erosion.  While the analysis is sound, it is not especially rigorous (e.g., no comparative ratios or sensitivity checks), so the work aligns with âadequately applied; relevance not always clearâ in band 3.

The student selects a limited but reasonable range of data: revenue, cost, gross profit, market-share percentages and user numbers.  Data are drawn from credible sources and incorporated into tables/graphics, fulfilling the âadequate selection and useâ descriptor (band 3).

Analysis explains why revenue rose, why GPM fell, and how force-field drivers outweighed restraints; it links these points to performance and competitive position, showing mostly effective reasoning.  Some integration between data sets is present, though not sustained throughout, matching âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ (band 4).
The student explicitly answers the research question (âimpacted â¦ to a greater extentâ with specific positives and negatives), satisfying the top-band requirement for an explicit answer.

The IA employs two business tools: Force-Field Analysis and Gross Profit Margin.  This satisfies âsome tools and theories selectedâ but does not reach the breadth implied by âadequate selectionâ.  Hence band 2 is most appropriate.

The concluding statements (revenue up 32 %, market share to 65 %, cost pressures) clearly mirror evidence presented in the analysis sections, indicating full consistency with the data.  This fits band 3, the highest available for this strand.

Throughout the IA, the analysis is clearly anchored to Alibaba: market-share figures, user growth, force-field drivers/restraints and the gross-profit table are all organisation-specific.  The link to Alibabaâs strategy and operations is explicit and is revisited in every analytical section, though depth could have been improved by examining pre- and post-acquisition synergy data in greater detail.  This demonstrates a strong, partially integrated organisational connection, consistent with band 4.

The student defines the concept of change, links it to acquisitions and repeatedly returns to âthe change brought by the acquisitionâ when discussing market-share growth, revenue shifts and cost pressures.  However, references to the concept are clustered mainly in the introduction, the Force-Field section and the conclusion; other sections (e.g. methodology, financial table discussion) treat change only implicitly.  The integration is therefore strong but not wholly sustained, matching the descriptor for band 4 rather than the fully-integrated descriptor for band 5.

annotations.comment: The table of contents provides a logical roadmap, but it lacks page numbers for quick navigation; consider adding them for clarity.
The inclusion of a clear structure via distinct headings demonstrates consistent organization throughout the IA.
The research question is clearly and succinctly stated, providing a focused inquiry that guides the entire assessment.
The key concept of âChangeâ is prominently identified, laying a solid foundation for analytical links throughout the IA.
The introduction offers detailed background but underplays how this context directly informs the research question; explicitly link to the acquisitionâs significance.
The definition of acquisition with date and value anchors the IA in context and aligns closely with the research focus on performance impact.
Numerical data on active users and revenue are presented without inline citations; add page or paragraph references to improve source traceability.
Linking the business management guide definition of Change to the IAâs scope illustrates good conceptual integration at the methodology stage.
The methodology outlines sources and tools but omits details on data selection criteria and analysis procedures; specify how and why particular data were chosen.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The description of Force Field Analysis is concise and clearly explains weighting, demonstrating appropriate selection of this tool.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Figure 1 lacks explicit numeric labels for driver and restraint weights, reducing transparency; include a table or legend with each factorâs score.
The analysis heading would benefit from restating the key concept âChangeâ to reinforce conceptual integration before diving into findings.
The section articulates both internal driving factors (market share, user growth) and external influences, showing effective data use.
While drivers and restraints are described, the analysis would be stronger with quantification of each force and discussion of sensitivity to weight changes.
Financial performance data are well presented in Table 1, demonstrating effective use of revenue and cost figures to assess impact.
Discussion attributes GPM decline broadly to inventory costs; isolate Kaola-related cost changes to strengthen causal analysis.
The source bias of Guo Yuyanâs article is not addressed; triangulate with Alibabaâs official reports to enhance reliability.
The conclusion synthesizes key findings and directly answers the research question, demonstrating full consistency with evidence.
The conclusion restates results but does not discuss limitations or uncertainties; include a reflection on data constraints.
A broad range of sources is cited, but exceeding five documents dilutes depth; prioritize the most impactful three to five references.
Reference formatting is inconsistent across DOIs and URLs; apply a uniform citation style for professionalism.
Inclusion of the TechCrunch acquisition report provides timely industry context supporting the analysis.
The marketingtochina.com source may lack academic rigor; supplement with peer-reviewed or official reports to deepen the evidence base.
The ResearchGate thesis is relevant but would benefit from integrating its quantitative insights directly into the analysis.
